title: tephroseris integrifolia subsp. atropurpurea
intro: Tephroseris integrifolia subsp. atropurpurea, also known as Purple Mountain Everlasting, is a plant species in the daisy family that is native to the mountainous regions of central and southern Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are derived from its essential oils and flavonoids. Tephroseris integrifolia subsp. atropurpurea has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
